title: daucus carota subsp. commutatus
intro: Daucus carota subsp. commutatus, commonly known as wild carrot, is a subspecies of the carrot plant that grows in the wild and has a more robust and branched root system compared to its cultivated counterpart. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, which make it effective in treating digestive issues, wounds, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Daucus carota subsp. commutatus include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce fever, and alleviate menstrual cramps, among others. Wild carrot can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and its dried root can also be used in capsule or powder form to harness its medicinal properties.
